result:
Here are the middleware files that check user permissions, grouped by function:

### Core Permission/Access Middleware

| File | Class/Function | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `app/Http/Middleware/apiMonitorMiddleware.php` | `apiMonitorMiddleware` | **Primary permission enforcer.** Key methods: `enforceAdminUserListingAccess()` (checks `MANAGE_USERS` permission via `usersPermissionsMapping` table), `enforceStoreAccess()` (validates user can access the requested store via `userStoreMapping`), `shouldBypassStoreAccess()` / `getStoreAccessBypassRules()` |
| `app/Http/Middleware/OauthMiddleware.php` | `OauthMiddleware` | OAuth2 token validation — verifies `access_token`, resolves `client_id`, aborts 403 on invalid tokens |
| `app/Http/Middleware/MyMiddleware.php` | `MyMiddleware` | Secondary OAuth token check — validates token in `accessToken` table, checks `isActive`, aborts 403 if unauthorized |
| `app/Http/Middleware/Authenticate.php` | `Authenticate` | Laravel's standard `Guard`-based auth — redirects unauthenticated users or returns 401 |

### Partner/Third-Party Access

| File | Class/Function | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `app/Http/Middleware/partnerAppToken.php` | `partnerAppToken` | Validates partner tokens against `globalPartnerAccessTokens` |
| `app/Http/Middleware/thirdPartyToken.php` | `thirdPartyToken` | Third-party API token validation |
| `app/Http/Middleware/UrbanpiperAccess.php` | `UrbanpiperAccess` | Urbanpiper-specific access control |
| `app/Http/Middleware/verifyMobiquest.php` | `verifyMobiquest` | Mobiquest integration auth — returns "You don't have permission" on failure |

### Domain-Specific Auth

| File | Class/Function | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `app/Http/Middleware/MerchantMiddleware.php` | `MerchantMiddleware` | Merchant-level auth |
| `app/Http/Middleware/EcomMiddleware.php` | `EcomMiddleware` | E-commerce route auth |
| `app/Http/Middleware/webMiddleware.php` / `webMiddlewareV2.php` | `webMiddleware` / `webMiddlewareV2` | Web dashboard auth |
| `app/Http/Middleware/NavisionMiddleware.php` | `NavisionMiddleware` | Navision ERP integration auth |

The **heaviest permission logic** is in `apiMonitorMiddleware.php` — it does both store-level access control and permission-based authorization (checking the `permissions` and `usersPermissionsMapping` tables).
